From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Co-creator of the Ultimate Fighting Championship

**Robert "Bob" Meyrowitz** is the co-creator of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") ([UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")).[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bob_Meyrowitz&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

In 1973, Meyrowitz was a founder of D.I.R. Broadcasting and a creator of the syndicated radio series, the [King Biscuit Flower Hour](/wiki/King_Biscuit_Flower_Hour "King Biscuit Flower Hour").

In the late 1980s he started Semaphore Entertainment Group (SEG), which was one of the first groups to develop content for [pay per view](/wiki/Pay_per_view "Pay per view").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 1999, Meyrowitz created eYada.com, an internet [talk radio](/wiki/Talk_radio "Talk radio") station, ceasing in 2001.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 2001, Meyrowitz was the owner of the UFC who sold it to [Frank Fertitta III](/wiki/Frank_Fertitta_III "Frank Fertitta III") and [Lorenzo Fertitta](/wiki/Lorenzo_Fertitta "Lorenzo Fertitta") ([Zuffa](/wiki/Zuffa "Zuffa")).[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In January 2008, Meyrowitz announced the formation of his new [MMA](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") company, YAMMA Pit Fighting.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) YAMMA was a joint venture between Meyrowitz's company, Rope Partners, and [Live Nation](/wiki/Live_Nation "Live Nation"). Yamma folded the same year, holding only one event on April 11, 2008.

On June 10, 2016, Meyrowitz was inducted to the Contributor wing of the [UFC Hall of Fame](/wiki/UFC_Hall_of_Fame "UFC Hall of Fame").[[11]](#cite_note-hof-11)
